channel.title: Quality3Ds Daily Pulse
channel.description: Curated daily news for 3D printing enthusiasts.

item.title: 3D PRINTING PULSE #71 - 2026-03-19
item.description:
🔥 DUT team develops microwave-laser hybrid method for 3D printed ceramics Researchers at Dalian University of Technology have developed a hybrid additive manufacturing technique that integrates a microwave field with laser-based 3D printing to address a persistent defect problem in ceramic... Reported by VoxelMatters on Mar 17, 2026. Image ? https://www.voxelmatters.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/03/DUT-hybrid-3D-print-method-ceramics_01-150x150.jpg Read more ? https://www.voxelmatters.com/dut-team-develops-microwave-laser-hybrid-method-for-3d-printed-ceramics

🔥 Vision Transformers Boost Real Time FFF Quality Monitoring Researchers propose a Vision Transformer approach that detects FFF surface defects in real time with on-demand explainability. The post Vision Transformers Boost Real Time FFF Quality Monitoring appeared on Fabbaloo. Read more ? https://www.fabbaloo.com/news/vision-transformers-boost-real-time-fff-quality-monitoring

🔥 Minnesota lawmakers target ‘ghost guns’ with new ban - Valley News Live Minnesota lawmakers target ‘ghost guns’ with new ban Valley News Live Reported by Google News - 3D Printing Viral on Mar 17, 2026. Read more ? https://news.google.?oc=5

♻️ Thermwood Gear Pump Patent Optimizes Large Format Extrusions A patent proposal by Thermwood could solve one of large area extrusion’s oldest challenges: keeping bead size consistent when a pellet fed printhead speeds up, slows down, and corners. The post Thermwood Gear Pump Patent Optimizes Large Format Extrusions appeared on Fabbaloo. Read more ? https://www.fabbaloo.com/news/thermwood-gear-pump-patent-optimizes-large-format-extrusions

🖨 EX3D Prints: your neighbor’s printer as a node in a global manufacturing network Millions of 3D printers around the world sit idle for most of the day. Charged, connected, and ready to go, reported by VoxelMatters on Mar 18, 2026. Image ? https://www.voxelmatters.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/03/ex3d01-150x150.png Read more ? https://www.voxelmatters.com/ex3d-prints-your-neighbors-printer-as-a-node-in-a-global-manufacturing-network

🚀 GE Aerospace commits €110 million to European expansion GE Aerospace has outlined plans to invest more than €110 million ($126.6m) across its European manufacturing sites in 2026, targeting production capacity expansion, advanced manufacturing acceleration, and stronger... Reported by VoxelMatters on Mar 18, 2026. Image ? https://www.voxelmatters.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/03/GE-Aerospace-March-2026_02-150x150.jpg Read more ? https://www.voxelmatters.com/ge-aerospace-commits-e110-million-to-european-expansion

🖨 Eplus3D teases new metal additive manufacturing system with 256 lasers Has the multi-laser additive manufacturing race reached its peak? Reported by TCT Magazine on Mar 18, 2026. Image ? https://www.tctmagazine.com/content/images/2026/03/1773814876431.jpeg Read more ? https://www.tctmagazine.com/eplus3d-teases-new-metal-additive-manufacturing-system-with-256-lasers

📰 Sintavia validates lightweight 3D printed heat exchanger in two weeks with NVIDIA GPUs "Because we operate in a fully digital environment, we are always looking at faster and more efficient solutions to reduce span time at each step." Reported by TCT Magazine on Mar 18, 2026. Image ? https://www.tctmagazine.com/content/images/2026/03/Sintavia_Press_Release_Image_31726.jpg Read more ? https://www.tctmagazine.com/sintavia-validates-lightweight-3d-printed-heat-exchanger-in-two-weeks-with-nvidia-gpus

item.title: 3D PRINTING PULSE #70 - 2026-03-18
item.description:
🔥 Viral Watch: "$100 3D-printed MANPADS launcher" video sparks global backlash A viral English-language video shows a low-cost launcher prototype and has triggered intense debate about safety, platform moderation, and responsible publishing of weapon-adjacent content. Watch source: https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=DDO2EvXyncE Project repository: https://github.com/novatic14/Distributed-Camera-Node-Tracking-System Read more ? https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=DDO2EvXyncE 🦺 Hot-wire Laser Additive Manufacturing Produces Defect-free WC–Co Cemented Carbide Researchers led by Keita Marumoto at Hiroshima University together with collaborators from Mitsubishi Materials Hardmetal Corporation, a manufacturer of cemented carbide tooling materials, investigated how laser... The results published in the International Journal of Refractory Metals and Hard Materials show that combining […], reported by 3D Printing Industry on Mar 16, 2026. Image ? https://3dprintingindustry.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/03/SEM-image-of-cemented-carbide-molded-with-the-rod‑leading-method.-Image-via-ScienceDirect-150x100.jpg Read more ? https://3dprintingindustry.com/news/hot-wire-laser-additive-manufacturing-produces-defect-free-wc-co-cemented-carbide-249946

🚀 PrintPal Launches Browser-based Tool Suite PrintPal, a 3D printing technology platform headquartered in Chicago, Illinois, has launched PrintPal Tools, a suite of more than 15 browser-based tools for hobbyists, professional makers, and online sellers. All tools run entirely in a browser and can be accessed without installing software or creating an account, reported by 3D Printing Industry on Mar 16, 2026. Image ? https://3dprintingindustry.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/03/flelele-1-150x100.jpg Read more ? https://3dprintingindustry.com/news/printpal-launches-browser-based-tool-suite-249934

🖨 Hanin debuts elastomer 3D printer at TCT Asia The LCD-L298 promises 'high precision and high efficiency for dual-component elastomer printing' and highlights potential applications across footwear, consumer electronics and more. Reported by TCT Magazine on Mar 17, 2026. Image ? https://www.tctmagazine.com/content/images/2026/03/Hanin-3D-printer.png Read more ? https://www.tctmagazine.com/hanin-debuts-elastomer-3d-printer-at-tct-asia

📰 NIDEC Machine Tool America rebrands AM division and consolidates three tech platforms NIDEC Machine Tool America has rebranded its Additive Manufacturing division as Advanced Manufacturing Technologies, consolidating three distinct precision manufacturing platforms under a single organizational unit. The restructured division brings together … The post NIDEC Machine Tool America rebrands AM division and consolidates three tech platforms appeared first on VoxelMatters - The heart of additive manufacturing. Image ? https://www.voxelmatters.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/03/NIDEC-MACHINE-TOOL-AMERICA-150x150.jpg Read more ? https://www.voxelmatters.com/nidec-machine-tool-america-rebrands-am-division-and-consolidates-three-tech-platforms

💻 B9Creations and Würth Additive Group Target the Quality Gap in Distributed Spare Parts Production B9Creations, a U.S.-based precision 3D printing manufacturer, and Würth Additive Group, the additive arm of Würth Group, have partnered to address one of industrial manufacturing’s most persistent headaches: keeping... The two companies announced a strategic partnership that combines B9Creations’ validated 3D printing technology, […], reported by 3D Printing Industry on Mar 17, 2026. Image ? https://3dprintingindustry.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/03/B9Creations-Lab-150x100.jpg Read more ? https://3dprintingindustry.com/news/b9creations-and-wurth-additive-group-target-the-quality-gap-in-distributed-spare-parts-production-249989

🖨 Metallic Glass Motor Components 3D Printed in Breakthrough for Energy-Efficient Electric Motors Electric motors are among the most widespread energy-converting devices on the planet, and among the least efficient at small scales. A measurable share of the electricity they consume is lost as heat, a consequence of how conventional metal alloys respond to alternating magnetic fields, reported by 3D Printing Industry on Mar 17, 2026. Image ? https://3dprintingindustry.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/03/PM_Ralf_Busch_links_Amirhossein_Ghavimi_rechts-150x100.jpeg Read more ? https://3dprintingindustry.com/news/metallic-glass-motor-components-3d-printed-in-breakthrough-for-energy-efficient-electric-motors-249978

🚀 Raven Series by 3DMakerPro Makes Professional-Grade LiDAR Scanning Accessible 3D scanner manufacturer 3DMakerPro is introducing the Raven series, a new line of LiDAR-based 3D scanners that deliver professional-grade accuracy in a portable, creator-friendly format. Featuring integrated LiDAR sensors and advanced imaging technology, the Raven is engineered to quickly and accurately capture rooms, scenes, and objects, generating high-quality 3D point cloud data ideal for, reported by 3D Printing Industry on Mar 16, 2026. Image ? https://3dprintingindustry.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/03/image-7-150x100.png Read more ? https://3dprintingindustry.com/news/raven-series-by-3dmakerpro-makes-professional-grade-lidar-scanning-accessible-249965

🖨 NIDEC Machine Tool America Rebrands AM Division as Advanced Manufacturing Technologies NIDEC Machine Tool America, a company that supplies gear-making machines and manufacturing equipment, has rebranded its Additive Manufacturing division as Advanced Manufacturing Technologies, bringing several... The division includes LAMDA, a laser powder directed energy deposition (LP-DED) metal additive manufacturing system; ABLASER, a laser micromachining platform; and BOND MEISTER, a […], reported by 3D Printing Industry on Mar 16, 2026. Image ? https://3dprintingindustry.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/03/NIDEC_Advanced_Manufacturing_Technologies_Lineup-150x100.png Read more ? https://3dprintingindustry.com/news/nidec-machine-tool-america-rebrands-am-division-as-advanced-manufacturing-technologies-249940

item.title: 3D PRINTING PULSE #69 - 2026-03-17
item.description:
🦺 Cairn Surgical submits De Novo 510(k) FDA application for 3D printing-enabled Breast Cancer Locator System A medical device can be designated for the De Novo pathway when it offers novel technology with a reasonable assurance of safety and effectiveness. Reported by TCT Magazine on Mar 16, 2026. Image ? https://www.tctmagazine.com/content/images/2026/03/bright-crop-scaled-1-1.png Read more ? https://www.tctmagazine.com/cairn-surgical-submits-de-novo-510-k-fda-application-for-3d-printing-enabled-breast-cancer-locator-system

🚀 Mechnano expands CNT tech to three flexible elastomers for pellet-based additive manufacturing "By enabling consistent CNT functionality across multiple material types and additive processes, they give engineers the flexibility to prototype functional components without being tied to a single hardware system.” Reported by TCT Magazine on Mar 16, 2026. Image ? https://www.tctmagazine.com/content/images/2026/03/Mechnano-Pellet-Elastomers.png Read more ? https://www.tctmagazine.com/mechnano-expands-cnt-tech-to-three-flexible-elastomers-for-pellet-based-additive-manufacturing

🏠 How I 3D Printed My Own Lego-Compatible Train Bridges Lego train sets have been available for decades, now. The Danish manufacturer long ago realized the magic of combining its building block sets with motors and plastic rails to create …read more, reported by Hackaday 3D Printing on Mar 16, 2026. Image ? https://hackaday.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/03/HAD-concept-2.jpg Read more ? https://hackaday.com/2026/03/16/how-i-3d-printed-my-own-lego-compatible-train-bridges

📰 Pete Pharma and Atrium24 partner to bring 3D printing to independents Pete Pharma, a pharmaceutical innovation and intellectual property development company focused on advanced 3D printing and proprietary drug delivery systems, has formed a strategic partnership with Atrium24... Reported by VoxelMatters on Mar 16, 2026. Image ? https://www.voxelmatters.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/03/Pete-Pharma-Atrium24-partnership-150x150.jpg Read more ? https://www.voxelmatters.com/pete-pharma-and-atrium24-partner-to-bring-3d-printing-to-independents

📰 Korean researchers turn petroleum refining waste into 4D printed soft robotics The crude oil refining process generates substantial waste and by-products such as elemental sulfur, which is typically converted into sulfuric acid and used as a fertilizer. However, we are at … The post Korean researchers turn petroleum refining waste into 4D printed soft robotics appeared first on VoxelMatters - The heart of additive manufacturing. Image ? https://www.voxelmatters.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/03/krict-4d-printing-1-150x150.jpeg Read more ? https://www.voxelmatters.com/korean-researchers-turn-petroleum-refining-waste-into-4d-printed-soft-robotics

🚀 ROBOZE launches ARGO 500 HYPERSPEED MISSION READY additive manufacturing platform ROBOZE says it has released the new product in response to increasingly complex supply chains and operations. Reported by TCT Magazine on Mar 16, 2026. Image ? https://www.tctmagazine.com/content/images/2026/03/argo-500-hyperspeed-mr-1-1.png Read more ? https://www.tctmagazine.com/roboze-launches-argo-500-hyperspeed-mission-ready-additive-manufacturing-platform

📰 UCSF researchers develop composite to standardize organoid development Scientists at the University of California, San Francisco have developed a new biomaterial that enables 3D printing of stem cells into precise configurations within petri dishes. The development addresses a … The post UCSF researchers develop composite to standardize organoid development appeared first on VoxelMatters - The heart of additive manufacturing. Image ? https://www.voxelmatters.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/03/UCSF-cells_01-e1773618396369-150x150.jpg Read more ? https://www.voxelmatters.com/ucsf-researchers-develop-composite-to-standardize-organoid-development

🚀 Nike launches first virtual 3D printed shoe in Fortnite with the Air Max 95000 Because of my professional background (10 years spent as a trade journalist in the video game industry), I love it when video games and 3D printing intersect. Usually, it’s about … The post Nike launches first virtual 3D printed shoe in Fortnite with the Air Max 95000 appeared first on VoxelMatters - The heart of additive manufacturing. Image ? https://www.voxelmatters.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/03/fortnite-nike-zellerfeld-150x150.jpg Read more ? https://www.voxelmatters.com/nike-launches-first-virtual-3d-printed-shoe-in-fortnite-with-the-air-max-95000

item.title: 3D PRINTING PULSE #68 - 2026-03-16
item.description:
🦺 #242 Inside XJet's new product & market strategy with CEO Guy Zimmerman Amnovis is a manufacturing production service that supplies parts to the medical and industrial sectors. Reported by TCT Magazine on Mar 16, 2026. Image ? https://www.tctmagazine.com/content/images/2026/03/Additive-Insight-Re-Design--17-.png Read more ? https://www.tctmagazine.com/podcasts/242-inside-xjets-new-product-market-strategy-with-ceo-guy-zimmerman

🚀 Nikon AM Synergy Secures US Department of War Contract to Qualify AM for Aeronautical Systems Nikon AM Synergy, the engineering and manufacturing arm of Nikon Advanced Manufacturing, has secured an Other Transition Agreement (OTA) contract with the U.S. Department of War’s Defense Innovation Unit (DIU) under the Foundry for Operational Readiness and Global Effects (FORGE) program, reported by 3D Printing Industry on Mar 16, 2026. Image ? https://3dprintingindustry.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/03/1773147342023-150x100.jpeg Read more ? https://3dprintingindustry.com/news/nikon-am-synergy-secures-us-department-of-war-contract-to-qualify-am-for-aeronautical-systems-249899

🏠 A Smart Printer Enclosure for the Open Source World 3D printing has had its time to spread its wings into the everyday home, yet many of those homes lack the proper ventilation to prevent the toxic VOCs from escaping. Reported by Hackaday 3D Printing on Mar 15, 2026. Image ? https://hackaday.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/03/HeroShot1_49e186.jpg Read more ? https://hackaday.com/2026/03/15/a-smart-printer-enclosure-for-the-open-source-world

📰 A large format portal to the future of composite 3D printing The biggest show in the world for the composites industry, JEC World, is a great place to see the latest developments in advanced manufacturing for some of the most advanced … The post A large format portal to the... Reported by VoxelMatters on Mar 16, 2026. Image ? https://www.voxelmatters.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/03/JEC-WORLD-2026-entrance-150x150.png Read more ? https://www.voxelmatters.com/an-lfam-platform-to-the-future-of-composite-3d-printing

🖨 America Makes and NCDMM Target Metal AM’s Corrosion Testing Problem America Makes and the National Center for Defense Manufacturing and Machining (NCDMM) have revealed the recipients of a newly funded initiative backed by the Office of the Under Secretary of Defense, Manufacturing... Totaling $1.3 million, the initiative, titled Corrosion of Additive – Tested At Component Scale (CATACS), targets one of additive […], reported by 3D Printing Industry on Mar 16, 2026. Image ? https://3dprintingindustry.com/wp-content/uploads/2025/09/America-Makes-facility-face-150x100.jpg Read more ? https://3dprintingindustry.com/news/america-makes-and-ncdmm-target-metal-ams-corrosion-testing-problem-249904

🚀 ICON launches Titan program to commercialize robotic 3D printing construction system for builders ICON, a Texas-based construction technology company developing large-scale 3D printing systems, has commercially launched its Titan robotic construction platform. Announced on March 11, 2026, the new Titan program allows builders and construction companies to acquire ICON’s integrated 3D printing construction system, marking the first time the company has offered its full, reported by 3D Printing Industry on Mar 16, 2026. Image ? https://3dprintingindustry.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/03/Operating-Titan_Photo-Credit-Casey-Dunn-150x100.jpg Read more ? https://3dprintingindustry.com/news/icon-launches-titan-program-to-commercialize-robotic-3d-printing-construction-system-for-builders-249907

💻 How F1 teams are using 3D printed pistons to circumvent compression rules Formula 1’s governing body, the FIA, introduced a revised compression ratio ceiling of 16:1 for the 2026 season, down from the previous limit of 18:1, with the aim of reducing … The post How F1 teams are using 3D... Reported by VoxelMatters on Mar 15, 2026. Image ? https://www.voxelmatters.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/03/3D-printed-F1-pistons_Mercedes_02-e1773614498464-150x150.jpg Read more ? https://www.voxelmatters.com/how-f1-teams-are-using-3d-printed-pistons-to-circumvent-compression-rules

📷 Affordable custom 3D printed cycling shoes use smartphone foot-scan technology Cycling shoes can be a cyclist’s best, or worst, friend. A great fit will make any ride a pleasure, but even the slightest of pinches or pressure in the wrong … The post Affordable custom 3D printed cycling shoes use smartphone foot-scan technology appeared first on, reported by VoxelMatters on Mar 15, 2026. Image ? https://www.voxelmatters.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/03/Hezo-cycling-shoes_07-150x150.jpg Read more ? https://www.voxelmatters.com/affordable-custom-3d-printed-cycling-shoes-use-smartphone-foot-scan-technology

item.title: 3D PRINTING PULSE #67 - 2026-03-15
item.description:
🚀 Intergalactic Turns to Velo3D to Accelerate Aircraft Heat Exchanger Development A new aviation project shows how metal 3D printing can dramatically shorten the time it takes to turn a design into a working aircraft component. Velo3D announced that aerospace supplier, reported by 3DPrint.com on Mar 13, 2026. Read more ? https://3dprint.com/324538/intergalactic-turns-to-velo3d-to-accelerate-aircraft-heat-exchanger-development

🏠 Urban village demolition waste turned into 3D printed street furniture A research-driven project called Inorganic Growth has converted construction waste sourced from demolished urban villages into 3D printed street furniture. The initiative, from studio Bentu Design, combined material reprocessing with … The post Urban village demolition waste turned into 3D printed street furniture appeared first on VoxelMatters - The heart of additive... Image ? https://www.voxelmatters.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/03/3D-printed-street-furniture_04-e1773481995104-150x150.jpg Read more ? https://www.voxelmatters.com/urban-village-demolition-waste-turned-into-3d-printed-street-furniture

🖨 Aconity3D releases multi-material welding head for AconityWIRE Aconity3D, a German company specializing in metal 3D printing, has released a multi-material welding head for its AconityWIRE directed energy deposition (DED) platform. The component makes the processing of up … The post Aconity3D releases multi-material welding head for AconityWIRE appeared first on VoxelMatters - The heart of additive manufacturing. Image ? https://www.voxelmatters.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/03/AconityWIRE_01-150x150.jpg Read more ? https://www.voxelmatters.com/aconity3d-releases-multi-material-welding-head-for-aconitywire

💻 TCT Asia 2026 Now Returns to Shanghai Bigger Than Ever Additive manufacturing has come a long way in a short time, and TCT Asia 2026 is a decent measure of that. When the show opens at the National Exhibition and Convention Center in Shanghai on March 17 to 19, it will do so with 550 exhibitors across 55,000 sq, reported by 3D Printing Industry on Mar 14, 2026. Image ? https://3dprintingindustry.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/03/f30be1e730064a1ba9825b3fa7352c8-150x100.jpg Read more ? https://3dprintingindustry.com/news/tct-asia-2026-now-returns-to-shanghai-bigger-than-ever-249912

📰 XTPL Sells First ODRA System to Silicon Valley Semiconductor Packaging Client One prerequisite for success in additive manufacturing (AM) is the establishment of a proven system for converting initial sales used as tech validation into future sales of higher-value hardware that’s... Reported by 3DPrint.com on Mar 13, 2026. Read more ? https://3dprint.com/324544/3d-printed-advanced-packaging-specialist-xtpl-sells-first-odra-system-to-silicon-valley-client

📰 Ursa Major, AFRL Show AM’s Role in Future Deterrence Through Draper Test Flight The war in Iran is only about two weeks old, but countless lessons — and warnings — have already emerged for militaries across the planet and the economy in general.... Reported by 3DPrint.com on Mar 13, 2026. Read more ? https://3dprint.com/324558/ursa-major-afrl-show-ams-role-in-future-deterrence-through-draper-test-flight

📰 Dallas, Texas Is a Drone and 3D Printing Place to Be Goulding and Preeti Sulibhavi explore how Dallas, Texas, has quietly become a national hotspot where drone manufacturing and 3D printing converge to drive rapid innovation, scalable production, and defense-focused... The post Dallas, Texas Is a Drone and 3D Printing Place to Be appeared on Fabbaloo. Read more ? https://www.fabbaloo.com/news/dallas-texas-is-a-drone-and-3d-printing-place-to-be

🖨 Ameralabs Publishes Comprehensive 23-Point Safety Analysis of 3D Printing Resin Ameralabs has published a fascinating analysis of the toxicity of 3D printer resin. The post Ameralabs Publishes Comprehensive 23-Point Safety Analysis of 3D Printing Resin appeared on Fabbaloo. Read more ? https://www.fabbaloo.com/news/ameralabs-publishes-comprehensive-23-point-safety-analysis-of-3d-printing-resin

item.title: 3D PRINTING PULSE #66 - 2026-03-14
item.description:
🦺 Future Form acquires HP Multi Jet Fusion 3D printer Future Form, a U.S. manufacturer producing precision-engineered components for the data center, medical, defense, nuclear, and aerospace industries, has introduced plastic 3D printing services to its manufacturing portfolio, reported by 3D Printing Industry on Mar 13, 2026. Image ? https://3dprintingindustry.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/03/Future_Form_HP_Multi_Jet_Fusion_MJF_3D_printing_system-150x100.jpg Read more ? https://3dprintingindustry.com/news/future-form-acquires-hp-multi-jet-fusion-3d-printer-249857

🚀 Prototype Integrates Bitcoin Mining ASICs into 3D Printer Bed A mechanical engineer based in North Dakota and known online as PizzAndy has developed a prototype 3D printer that replaces conventional resistive bed heating with Bitcoin mining ASIC chips. Showcased on the Home Mining Podcast, the system embeds mining hardware beneath the print surface so that hashing activity generates heat while simultaneously mining Bitcoin, reported by 3D Printing Industry on Mar 13, 2026. Image ? https://3dprintingindustry.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/03/PizzAndy-150x100.png Read more ? https://3dprintingindustry.com/news/prototype-integrates-bitcoin-mining-asics-into-3d-printer-bed-249862

🏠 3D printed metallic glass alloys aim to reduce EV motor losses Researchers at Saarland University have developed iron-rich amorphous alloys for electric motor components, an advancement which could help electric vehicles reduce energy consumption and operate for longer periods. The alloys … The post 3D printed metallic glass alloys aim to reduce EV motor losses appeared first on VoxelMatters - The heart of additive manufacturing. Image ? https://www.voxelmatters.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/03/Metallic-glass-in-EVs_01-150x150.jpg Read more ? https://www.voxelmatters.com/saarland-university-develops-3d-printed-metallic-glass-alloys-to-reduce-ev-motor-losses

📰 America Makes announces $1.3M CATACS selection America Makes and the National Center for Defense Manufacturing and Machining (NCDMM) have selected project teams for the Corrosion of Additive – Tested At Component Scale (CATACS) program. The $1.3 … The post America Makes announces $1.3M CATACS selection appeared first on VoxelMatters - The heart of additive manufacturing. Image ? https://www.voxelmatters.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/01/America-Makes-facility-face-150x150.jpg Read more ? https://www.voxelmatters.com/america-makes-announces-1-3m-catacs-selection

📰 Over 500 exhibitors from around the globe register for Formnext 2026 While the event is still eight months away, preparations are very much underway for Formnext 2026 in Frankfurt, Germany. The major AM trade show, which last year celebrated its 10th … The post Over 500 exhibitors from around the globe register for Formnext 2026 appeared first on VoxelMatters - The heart of additive manufacturing. Image ? https://www.voxelmatters.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/03/formnext-1-150x150.jpeg Read more ? https://www.voxelmatters.com/over-500-exhibitors-from-around-the-globe-register-for-formnext-2026

📰 USF using 3D printed clay structures to restore oyster populations Students and faculty at the University of South Florida (USF) have installed 3D printed clay structures along the seawall of Bayboro Harbor in St. Petersburg, Florida, as part of a … The post USF using 3D printed clay structures to restore oyster populations appeared first on VoxelMatters - The heart of additive manufacturing. Image ? https://www.voxelmatters.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/03/oyster-restoration-brick-structures_01-150x150.jpg Read more ? https://www.voxelmatters.com/usf-using-3d-printed-clay-structures-to-restore-oyster-populations

🚀 Addiguru Expands Collaborations to Study In-situ Monitoring and Defect Detection Addiguru, a company that develops multi-sensor monitoring systems for metal additive manufacturing, has announced a series of collaborations with manufacturers and research organisations aimed at improving defect... Partners include Apex Additive Technologies, Renishaw, Additive Manufacturing Solutions (AMS), LISI AEROSPACE Additive Manufacturing, The Manufacturing Technology Centre (MTC), and the Centre for […], reported by 3D Printing Industry on Mar 13, 2026. Image ? https://3dprintingindustry.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/03/addiguru-150x100.png Read more ? https://3dprintingindustry.com/news/addiguru-expands-collaborations-to-study-in-situ-monitoring-and-defect-detection-249868

🖨 Application spotlight: Lead time of automotive upright reduced from 4 days to 4 hours with metal additive manufacturing Cognitive Design Systems software provided a clearer view of the cost implications of using AM. Reported by TCT Magazine on Mar 13, 2026. Image ? https://www.tctmagazine.com/content/images/2026/03/Untitled-design--18--1.png Read more ? https://www.tctmagazine.com/application-spotlight-lead-time-of-automotive-upright-reduced-from-4-days-to-4-hours-with-metal-additive-manufacturing

item.title: 3D PRINTING PULSE #65 - 2026-03-13
item.description:
🦺 Ter Hoek Installs XJet Carmel 1400C to Enter Technical Ceramic Manufacturing Ter Hoek, a precision manufacturing specialist based in Netherlands, has installed the XJet Carmel 1400C ceramic 3D printing system. The move brings NanoParticle Jetting (NPJ) technology, developed by Israeli firm XJet, into one of Europe’s established precision manufacturing environments, with target applications in aerospace, semiconductor, and, reported by 3D Printing Industry on Mar 12, 2026. Image ? https://3dprintingindustry.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/03/Image-2-1-150x100.png Read more ? https://3dprintingindustry.com/news/ter-hoek-installs-xjet-carmel-1400c-to-enter-technical-ceramic-manufacturing-249824

🚀 New Aibuild OS automates engineering lifecycle from CAD to CAM UK-based AM software company Aibuild has introduced Aibuild OS, an advanced AI-based operating system that automates the engineering lifecycle, from CAD to CAM. The OS uses agentic AI to deliver … The post New Aibuild OS automates engineering lifecycle from CAD to CAM appeared first on VoxelMatters - The heart of additive manufacturing. Image ? https://www.voxelmatters.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/03/Aibuild-OS-3-150x150.jpg Read more ? https://www.voxelmatters.com/aibuild-os-automates-engineering-lifecycle-cad-cam

🏠 UK care provider exploring nutrient-rich 3D printed food Spellman Care, a company that operates a series of nursing homes for elderly residents in Yorkshire, UK, has teamed up with the University of Leeds to examine the use of … The post UK care provider exploring... Reported by VoxelMatters on Mar 12, 2026. Image ? https://www.voxelmatters.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/03/Carehome-3D-printed-food_02-150x150.jpg Read more ? https://www.voxelmatters.com/uk-care-provider-exploring-nutrient-rich-3d-printed-food

♻️ Why Recycling 3D Printed Plastic Remains Difficult Despite Growing Interest Everyone wants to recycle their 3D prints, but it is quite difficult to do so. The post Why Recycling 3D Printed Plastic Remains Difficult Despite Growing Interest appeared on Fabbaloo. Read more ? https://www.fabbaloo.com/news/why-recycling-3d-printed-plastic-remains-difficult-despite-growing-interest

💻 How additive manufacturing can realise the promise of AI at production scale Tyler Bouchard and Tyler Modelski explore the fundamental changes needed to scale industrial artificial intelligence in additive manufacturing. Reported by TCT Magazine on Mar 12, 2026. Image ? https://www.tctmagazine.com/content/images/2026/03/tct-featured-article.jpg Read more ? https://www.tctmagazine.com/how-additive-manufacturing-can-realise-the-promise-of-ai-at-production-scale

🚀 Formlabs officially launches Form Now 3D printing service with two-day US delivery Formlabs officially launched Form Now, its previously announced commercial 3D printing service. Form Now gives customers direct access to the company’s industrial stereolithography and selective laser sintering machines without requiring … The post Formlabs officially launches Form Now 3D printing service with, reported by VoxelMatters on Mar 12, 2026. Image ? https://www.voxelmatters.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/03/Formlabs_Form_Now_-2026-03-12-alle-15-1.19.44-e1773327174726-150x150.jpg Read more ? https://www.voxelmatters.com/formlabs-officially-launches-form-now-3d-printing-service-with-two-day-domestic-delivery

🖨 University of Strathclyde researchers 3D print super-resolution microscopy lenses Researchers at the University of Strathclyde have produced high-quality optical lenses using consumer-grade 3D printers, which demonstrate performance comparable to commercial optics for super-resolution fluorescence... The university reported it … The post University of Strathclyde researchers 3D print super-resolution microscopy lenses appeared first on VoxelMatters - The heart of additive manufacturing. Image ? https://www.voxelmatters.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/03/Uni-Strathclyde-microscopic-lens_02-150x150.jpg Read more ? https://www.voxelmatters.com/university-of-strathclyde-researchers-3d-print-super-resolution-microscopy-lenses

🚀 ICON launches Titan, its first commercially available multi-story construction 3D printer Texas-based ICON opened reservations for Titan, a multi-story robotic construction system the Austin company made available for purchase by builders and construction firms for the first time. The program marks … The post ICON launches Titan, its first commercially available multi-story construction 3D printer appeared first on VoxelMatters - The heart of additive manufacturing. Image ? https://www.voxelmatters.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/03/ICON_s-Titan_Photo-Credit-Casey-Dunn-150x150.jpg Read more ? https://www.voxelmatters.com/icon-launches-titan-its-first-commercially-available-multi-story-construction-3d-printer

item.title: 3D PRINTING PULSE #64 - 2026-03-12
item.description:
🦺 Leitat advances personalized medical device capabilities through DiMεD network Leitat Technological Center strengthened its capabilities in the development of personalized invasive medical devices through the Cervera DiMεD Network of Excellence, a project financed by Spain’s CDTI (Centro para... Reported by VoxelMatters on Mar 11, 2026. Image ? https://www.voxelmatters.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/03/DiMεD_Leitat_1772788192626-150x150.jpg Read more ? https://www.voxelmatters.com/leitat-advances-personalized-medical-device-capabilities-through-dim%ce%b5d-network

🚀 Addiguru enters & expands several partnerships to advance adoption of in-situ monitoring technology Renishaw, Apex Additive, MTC, LISI Aerospace & the University of Bolton are all working with Addiguru. Reported by TCT Magazine on Mar 11, 2026. Image ? https://www.tctmagazine.com/content/images/2026/03/Authentise-and-Addiguru.png Read more ? https://www.tctmagazine.com/addiguru-enters-expands-several-partnerships-to-advance-adoption-of-in-situ-monitoring-technology

🏠 ACMI installs AMCM M 8K metal 3D printing system American Center for Manufacturing and Innovation (ACMI), a U.S. organization focused on strengthening domestic manufacturing through investments in emerging industrial companies and infrastructure, has acquired an AMCM M 8K metal 3D printing system, reported by 3D Printing Industry on Mar 11, 2026. Image ? https://3dprintingindustry.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/03/AMCM_M8K-150x100.jpg Read more ? https://3dprintingindustry.com/news/acmi-installs-amcm-m-8k-metal-3d-printing-system-249752

🚀 Texas A&M and U.S. Army Develop Foam That Absorbs Ten Times More Energy Researchers at Texas A&M University and the DEVCOM Army Research Laboratory have engineered a composite that absorbs up to ten times more energy than conventional padding, threading a 3D printed elastomeric skeleton... The implications stretch well beyond […], reported by 3D Printing Industry on Mar 11, 2026. Image ? https://3dprintingindustry.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/03/20251103_Mohammad_Naraghi_Lab_AT_0016-1408x792-1-150x100.jpg Read more ? https://3dprintingindustry.com/news/texas-am-and-u-s-army-develop-foam-that-absorbs-ten-times-more-energy-249780

📰 EPFL develops 3D printable bone scaffold using enzyme-driven mineralization Researchers at the École Polytechnique Fédérale de Lausanne (EPFL) have developed a method to 3D print porous scaffolds made from hydroxyapatite (HA), which is the primary mineral component of bone. … The post EPFL develops 3D printable bone scaffold using enzyme-driven mineralization appeared first on VoxelMatters - The heart of additive manufacturing. Image ? https://www.voxelmatters.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/03/Cylindrical-demonstrations-of-the-3D-printed-scaffolds-150x150.jpg Read more ? https://www.voxelmatters.com/epfl-develops-3d-printable-bone-scaffold-using-enzyme-driven-mineralization

🖨 New IP Case Against Bambu Lab Heads to Shanghai Court In its latest legal challenge, 3D printer manufacturer Bambu Lab has been sued by Chinese toymaker Pop Mart over copyright infringement claims tied to knockoff Labubu files that were uploaded to Bambu Lab’s... The suit was filed at the People’s Court of Pudong New Area in Shanghai, with the trial scheduled for […], reported by 3D Printing Industry on Mar 11, 2026. Image ? https://3dprintingindustry.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/03/bb8bbb3f-0c66-4e8f-ac6a-d3ac4d7683cd_edd804f5-150x100.jpg Read more ? https://3dprintingindustry.com/news/new-ip-case-against-bambu-lab-heads-to-shanghai-court-249801

🦺 GELITA and Black Drop collaborate on GelMA bioinks for 3D bioprinting research GELITA, a German manufacturer of collagen proteins and gelatin used in food, pharmaceutical, and biomedical applications, has signed a research and development agreement with Black Drop, a biotechnology company... The collaboration will focus on developing bioinks for biomedical uses, including three-dimensional tissue models and implants, reported by 3D Printing Industry on Mar 11, 2026. Image ? https://3dprintingindustry.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/03/GELITA_BlackDrop_1_med-150x100.png Read more ? https://3dprintingindustry.com/news/gelita-and-black-drop-collaborate-on-gelma-bioinks-for-3d-bioprinting-research-249746

🚀 America Makes launches two DoD additive manufacturing project calls worth over $35M America Makes, the U.S. national additive manufacturing innovation institute, and the National Center for Defense Manufacturing and Machining (NCDMM) have announced two new project calls totaling more than $35 million in funding, reported by 3D Printing Industry on Mar 11, 2026. Image ? https://3dprintingindustry.com/wp-content/uploads/2025/09/America-Makes-facility-face-150x100.jpg Read more ? https://3dprintingindustry.com/news/america-makes-launches-two-dod-additive-manufacturing-project-calls-worth-over-35m-249741

item.title: 3D PRINTING PULSE #63 - 2026-03-11
item.description:
🦺 WSU researchers develop beating, 3D printed heart model for surgical training Researchers at Washington State University (WSU) have developed a 3D printed model of the left side of the heart that contracts and beats, giving surgeons and medical students a reusable, … The post WSU researchers... Reported by VoxelMatters on Mar 10, 2026. Image ? https://www.voxelmatters.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/03/3d-printing-of-heart-model-e1773137957718-150x150.jpg Read more ? https://www.voxelmatters.com/wsu-researchers-develop-beating-3d-printed-heart-model-for-surgical-training

🚀 Lithoz launching three materials at ceramitec 2026 Ceramic AM pioneer Lithoz is preparing for a big showcase at the upcoming ceramitec 2026 expo. Not only will the Austrian company be bringing a number of demonstrator components—including 46 … The post Lithoz launching three materials at ceramitec 2026 appeared first on VoxelMatters - The heart of additive... Image ? https://www.voxelmatters.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/03/LITHOZ_Zirconia_Chainmail-150x150.jpg Read more ? https://www.voxelmatters.com/lithoz-launching-upgraded-materials-ceramitec-2026

♻️ LAMA brings LFAM of recycled plastics to Latin America with robotic extrusion system LAMA, a Santiago-based company founded in August 2025, is using industrial robotic arms fitted with specialized polymer extruders from the Italian company REV3RD to produce large-format design objects and urban … The... Reported by VoxelMatters on Mar 10, 2026. Image ? https://www.voxelmatters.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/03/LAMA_3D_Chile_LFAM_IMG_5839-2-150x150.jpg Read more ? https://www.voxelmatters.com/lama-brings-large-scale-recycled-plastic-3d-printing-to-latin-america-with-robotic-extrusion-platform

📰 rpm and amsight develop 3D printed padding system for EOD helmets Additive manufacturing is being extensively deployed in military applications, across a range of equipment, vehicle parts, UAVs, and much more. Safety gear is one such area, and rapid product manufacturing … The post rpm and amsight develop 3D printed padding system for EOD helmets appeared first on VoxelMatters - The heart of additive manufacturing. Image ? https://www.voxelmatters.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/03/Safety-critical-helmet-padding-using-3D-printing-150x150.jpg Read more ? https://www.voxelmatters.com/rpm-and-amsight-develop-3d-printed-padding-system-for-eod-helmets

🚀 Nikon AM signs U.S. Defense Innovation Unit contract to reduce production bottlenecks with 3D printing Department of War (DoW) Defense Innovation Unit (DIU) is aiming to tackle production bottlenecks for its aeronautical systems using Nikon Advanced Manufacturing's metal additive manufacturing (AM) technology. The company's Nikon AM Synergy arm, which provides engineering and manufacturing services from its technology centre, reported by TCT Magazine on Mar 10, 2026. Image ? https://www.tctmagazine.com/content/images/2026/03/Nikon-AM-Synergy-Long-Beach-front.JPEG Read more ? https://www.tctmagazine.com/nikon-am-signs-u-s-defense-innovation-unit-contract-to-reduce-production-bottlenecks-with-3d-printing

🖨 [INTERVIEW] Creality CEO pitches long-term growth story as it prepares for IPO Desktop 3D printing has grown quickly, but its place in everyday life remains uncertain. As Creality moves towards a public listing, its founder is asking investors to look past product cycles and short-term results to a harder question: whether the market itself can still expand, reported by 3D Printing Industry on Mar 10, 2026. Image ? https://3dprintingindustry.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/03/Creality-at-Formnext-2025.-Photo-by-Michael-Petch-150x100.jpg Read more ? https://3dprintingindustry.com/news/interview-creality-ceo-pitches-long-term-growth-story-as-it-prepares-for-ipo-249770

📰 3D Printed plug-and-play Packaging Enables low-loss Fiber Coupling for Photonic Chips Researchers led by Erik Jung from Heidelberg University, a German research university with programs in photonics and nanotechnology, together with collaborators including Wolfram Pernice from University of Münster, a... Reported by 3D Printing Industry on Mar 10, 2026. Image ? https://3dprintingindustry.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/03/Plug-and-play-concept.-Photo-via-Science-150x100.jpg Read more ? https://3dprintingindustry.com/news/3d-printed-plug-and-play-packaging-enables-low-loss-fiber-coupling-for-photonic-chips-249763

🚀 3D People deploys AMIS Runtime to automate additive manufacturing build preparation UK additive manufacturing service provider 3D People has deployed AMIS Runtime, a programmable build preparation engine, across its London production facility. Announced on March 3, 2026, the deployment integrates automated build preparation into the company’s polymer powder bed fusion production workflows, reported by 3D Printing Industry on Mar 10, 2026. Image ? https://3dprintingindustry.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/02/FrontImage_AMIS_Runtime-150x100.png Read more ? https://3dprintingindustry.com/news/3d-people-deploys-amis-runtime-to-automate-additive-manufacturing-build-preparation-249738

item.title: 3D PRINTING PULSE #62 - 2026-03-10
item.description:
🦺 VoxelMatters Medical AM Focus 2026 eBook As one of the biggest adopters of additive manufacturing, the medical sector is always on our radar and our annual eBooks on the segment reflect that. This year, we are … The post VoxelMatters Medical AM Focus 2026 eBook appeared first on VoxelMatters - The heart of additive manufacturing. Image ? https://www.voxelmatters.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/03/Medical-AM-2026-eBook-Feature-Image-150x150.png Read more ? https://www.voxelmatters.com/voxelmatters-medical-am-focus-2026-ebook

🚀 Technical Review: Evaluating 3D Fuel Pro PCTG for Engineering FDM Applications This article presents a technical evaluation by the 3D Printing Industry engineering team of 3D Fuel’s Pro PCTG, a high-performance thermoplastic filament developed for FDM printing. Pro PCTG is a glycol-modified copolyester engineered to deliver improved toughness, dimensional stability, chemical resistance, and lower moisture sensitivity compared to standard PET and PETG, reported by 3D Printing Industry on Mar 09, 2026. Image ? https://3dprintingindustry.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/03/Benchmark-Test-Standard-Print-Speed--150x100.png Read more ? https://3dprintingindustry.com/news/technical-review-evaluating-3d-fuel-pro-pctg-for-engineering-fdm-applications-249716

♻️ Airtech to showcase new sustainable composite materials and 3D printed tooling at JEC World 2026 Airtech will present an expanded portfolio of sustainable materials and large-format additive manufacturing applications at JEC World 2026, demonstrating how 3D printed tooling and recycled composite resins can... Reported by VoxelMatters on Mar 09, 2026. Image ? https://www.voxelmatters.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/03/Airtec_JEC2026_-2026-03-05-alle-17.08.11-1-scaled-e1773053964156-150x150.jpg Read more ? https://www.voxelmatters.com/airtech-to-showcase-new-sustainable-composite-materials-and-3d-printed-tooling-at-jec-world-2026

🖨 INTERVIEW: Will Perseus Materials’ Bet on Self-propagating Chemistry be “world’s fastest” Composite Manufacturing Technology? A Stanford-spinout startup is developing a continuous composite manufacturing process it says can produce structural parts larger than the machine making them, using a self-propagating chemical reaction to eliminate... The chemistry originated at the California university, where researchers were working on a chemically recyclable resin for composite […], reported by 3D Printing Industry on Mar 09, 2026. Image ? https://3dprintingindustry.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/03/The-Perseus-Materials-team-at-JEC.-Photo-via-Perseus-Materials-150x100.png Read more ? https://3dprintingindustry.com/news/interview-will-perseus-materials-bet-on-self-propagating-chemistry-be-worlds-fastest-composite-manufacturing-technology-249731

📰 Lithoz presents three ceramic 3D printing material updates The company, which specialises in Lithography-based Ceramic Manufacturing, will present the updates at Ceramitec. Reported by TCT Magazine on Mar 09, 2026. Image ? https://www.tctmagazine.com/content/images/2026/03/LITHOZ_Alumina-and-ATZ_Heartpump-1.jpg Read more ? https://www.tctmagazine.com/lithoz-presents-three-ceramic-3d-printing-material-updates

🚀 Kaneko Optical Launches UROKO, a New 3D Printed Eyewear Collection for ISSEY MIYAKE MEN SS26 Kaneko Optical, a Japanese eyewear brand, has used 3D printing to translate the surface language of Japanese ceramics into wearable form, releasing UROKO, a new limited eyewear model for Japanese designer ISSEY... Eight Lenses, One Ceramic Reference UROKO departs […], reported by 3D Printing Industry on Mar 09, 2026. Image ? https://3dprintingindustry.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/03/Screenshot-2026-03-09-at-15.57.39-150x100.png Read more ? https://3dprintingindustry.com/news/kaneko-optical-launches-uroko-a-new-3d-printed-eyewear-collection-for-issey-miyake-men-ss26-249704

🖨 Stratasys Achieves Major Reliability Milestone with Industrial Partners 3D printer OEM Stratasys has reported a 22% year-over-year increase in system reliability for its F900 industrial printer, the result of targeted manufacturing upgrades rolled out in 2025 alongside its industrial... The improvements tackled production-scale obstacles directly, giving customers a stronger foundation to expand additive manufacturing into end-use parts and real-world […], reported by 3D Printing Industry on Mar 09, 2026. Image ? https://3dprintingindustry.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/03/STRP050_F900_with_part_transparent_bkg-150x100.jpg Read more ? https://3dprintingindustry.com/news/stratasys-achieves-major-reliability-milestone-with-industrial-partners-249699

🖨 Ter Hoek places faith in XJet for expansion into ceramics Ter Hoek, a precision manufacturing specialist based in Rijssen, Netherlands, has acquired an XJet Carmel 1400C ceramic 3D printing system, as it expands its production capabilities from metal-based processes into …... Reported by VoxelMatters on Mar 09, 2026. Image ? https://www.voxelmatters.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/03/XJet-1400C-Alumina-system-installed-at-Ter-Hoek-facility-in-Rijssen-The-Netherlands-e1773071474477-150x150.jpg Read more ? https://www.voxelmatters.com/ter-hoek-places-faith-in-xjet-for-expansion-into-ceramics
